text.title: personas Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for personas as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Nobody uses the personas after creation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Personas built without a plan for integration into design reviews, user stories, and prioritization are posters. Usage only follows if the team commits to referencing them in rituals.

failure-mode.description: Personas consist only of demographic data (age, role, location) with no goals, behaviors, or frustrations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Demographics tell you nothing about what to build. Goals and behaviors are the design levers; demographic data is context at best.

failure-mode.description: More than 5-6 primary personas exist for a single product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Teams cannot hold more than ~5 personas in working memory. Excess personas cause designers to default to their own mental model because the persona set is too large to use.

failure-mode.description: Personas were created once and never updated despite product pivots or new markets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stale personas mislead. A persona describing a 2021 user in a product that has since expanded to a new geography is worse than no persona — it is confident misinformation.

failure-mode.description: Personas created from team assumptions without any user research.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assumption-based personas reinforce existing biases rather than revealing user reality. They feel validated because they look like real personas.

failure-mode.description: Quote fields contain synthesized, "voicey" paraphrases invented by the author rather than verbatim participant quotes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic quotes tank credibility at first audit and are indistinguishable from hallucination. Use verbatim quotes with anonymous participant IDs only.

failure-mode.description: LLM generates plausible quotes and demographics without grounding them in source transcripts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate fluent, contextually appropriate-sounding quotes. Locking the drafter to evidence-only mode and rejecting outputs with quotes not in the source corpus prevents this.

failure-mode.description: Persona names and photos encode demographic stereotypes (always-female-marketer, always-male-CTO).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stereotype-encoded personas bias design decisions and are embarrassing in stakeholder reviews. Audit the persona set for diversity and rotate names and photos per update cycle.

failure-mode.description: Agent merges research data from different markets or locales into a single persona without explicit instruction.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cultural assumptions embedded in one-market data (US tech-comfortable manager) do not transfer across markets. Segment by locale first, create personas second.
